title: Tupa columnaris
intro: Tupa columnaris, also known as Tupa, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Tropaeolaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Tupa columnaris include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Tupa is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions.
